dialogueChunks.intro: They finished it, {player_name}. {quest_focus} is complete. And it's the most remarkable thing anyone here has ever seen.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The strange thing is what happened after. They set it down, stared at it for a long time, and then walked away. Went straight to find the people they'd pushed away. {target_npc} says they talked for hours. Whether those relationships survive, nobody knows yet. But for the first time in years, the workshop is dark.
dialogueChunks.outro: You gave them the means to finish. Whether that was mercy or enabling, time will tell. {quest_ally} says the result is worth a fortune. They haven't tried to sell it.

playerResponses.text: Sometimes you have to reach the end of something to find your way back.
I hope {quest_focus} was worth everything it cost.

dialogueChunks.intro: They succeeded. {quest_focus} works. It does exactly what they said it would. And they have absolutely no idea what to do next.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They sat in the wreckage and said: 'Now what?' Years of work, every relationship sacrificed, their health ruined, and the result is real. {target_npc} asked if it was worth it. They said: 'Ask me in ten years, when I know whether anyone I loved is still willing to speak to me.'
dialogueChunks.outro: The discovery will change lives, {player_name}. Just not theirs. Not yet. {quest_ally} is helping share the findings.

playerResponses.text: Genius and loneliness seem to travel together. I hope they find their way back.
The world will benefit even if they don't.
They chose the work over the people. That's nobody's fault but theirs.

dialogueChunks.intro: The final piece fit. {quest_focus} is complete. And they sat on the floor and wept for twenty minutes.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Not from joy. From looking around the empty room and realizing what surrounded them: achievements and dust instead of family. Their family came back when they heard. This time, they held the people close. {target_npc} says they haven't touched {quest_focus} since.
dialogueChunks.outro: Finding the last piece broke the spell, {player_name}. They can see what they lost now. That's either the beginning of recovery or the beginning of grief. Maybe they're the same thing.

playerResponses.text: They're holding their family instead of their work. That's a start.
Years of damage don't undo overnight. But at least they're trying.
